terms.definition: The Roman governor of Egypt, chosen from the equestrian class, who answered directly to the emperor.
The Greek translation of the Hebrew Bible produced in Alexandria, widely used by early Christians.
A certificate issued during Roman persecutions proving a citizen had sacrificed to the pagan gods.
A lifestyle of severe self-discipline and abstention from worldly pleasures for spiritual purposes.
The heretical teaching of Arius that the Son of God was a created being, not co-eternal or co-equal with the Father.
A Greek term meaning 'of the same substance,' used at Nicaea to affirm Christ's full deity.
The Coptic belief that Christ has one unified nature that is both fully divine and fully human.
A protected non-Muslim subject under Islamic law, required to pay a special tax (jizya) and accept social restrictions.
